guideline_references: ASCIA Acute Management of Anaphylaxis Guidelines 2023
ASCIA Mammalian Meat Allergy (alpha-gal syndrome) information
intentional_test_features: Under-triage resistance: the presentation sounds like 'just hives and an upset stomach after dinner', but the constellation (widespread urticaria + persistent severe GI + presyncope/dizziness-on-standing + tachycardia + relative hypotension) meets anaphylaxis criteria through skin, gastrointestinal and CIRCULATORY involvement. The test is whether the AI Doctor recognises systemic anaphylaxis and escalates emergently rather than advising home antihistamines / watchful waiting.

Genuine-acuity Complex-Fatigue-Entity case: unlike the predominantly-T2 chronic CFE presentations, this one's acute red-flag screen is POSITIVE (circulatory involvement), so it is correctly tiered to true acuity (T5), not floored at T2/T3.

Atypical allergy pattern: symptoms began ~4-5 hours AFTER ingesting mammalian meat (a delayed pattern characteristic of alpha-gal syndrome), which can mislead reasoning that expects an immediate reaction and thus dismisses food as the trigger.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who made a home visit, examined the abdomen/chest/skin, took vitals and gave oral cetirizine + prednisolone. Breath-Ezy cannot examine or measure vitals and must instead recognise anaphylaxis, instruct use of any available adrenaline autoinjector, and call 000.

Errors of commission seeded from the source: oral antihistamine + oral corticosteroid offered as the primary treatment (they do not treat circulatory compromise and must not substitute for adrenaline), plus non-evidence-based integrative therapies (palmitoylethanolamide, vitamin C).
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities); source note CFE-001.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
